summary: Batch is mathematically healthy with strong exit-side coverage as requested; primary concerns are sample-size starvation on triple-AND entries (#9, #10, #12), a literal weekday=Mon defect that contradicts stated intent (#14), and cross-channel archetypes (#17, #18) whose temporal/co-firing logic falls outside SUPPORTED_FIELDS so they degenerate to plain filters at parse time; #20 basis-invert and #5 session-boundary need transition vs state-predicate clarification but remain useful narrowings.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Flag-token exit (top_trader_position_ratio ≤ p25) is a smart-money rip-cord; orthogonal to PnL-shape kernels (fixed/trail/multi_tp/breakeven/time_only). Adjacent to existing X083 (exit_on_top_trader_change) but uses a state-flag rather than a delta-event.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: Applies to all open positions; ~25% of trade lives will cross top_trader_position_ratio into the bottom quartile post-entry (≈ 6000 trades/30d × 0.25 = 1500)
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Best as watchdog overlay on fixed kernel; weak signal added to breakeven; redundant with multi_tp ladder.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Differs from X080 (exit_on_funding_flip): exits when funding_abs leaves the high band (mean reversion) rather than on a sign flip. New regime-state exit family.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: Funding cadence is 8h on Binance → ≤3 evaluation points/day per trade; ~10% of high-funding entries see normalization within trade window. 6000 trades × 0.10 = 600
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Only meaningful when entry was conditioned on a funding-extreme regime; pairs with funding_abs_high entries; weakly informative for non-funding-conditioned entries.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Sentiment-flip exit gated on global_long_ratio_high transition out; current 133 exits are price/time-based and don't use crowd-sentiment regimes.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: Long-only subset (~50% of trades = 3000) × ~25% see global_long_ratio leave the p75+ band during trade window (8h funding cycles) ≈ 750-800
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Long-only family; pairs naturally with squeeze-long entries that conditioned on high crowd-long sentiment to begin with.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Differs from X081 (exit_on_premium_spike): exits on decay OUT of premium-extreme rather than on entry into one. State-leaving rather than state-entering — distinct kernel direction.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: Long-only subset 3000 × ~13% see premium leave the p90+ band within trade lifetime ≈ 400
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Best on long entries with premium_extreme in entry conditions; on other entries this is a near-no-op.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Calendar-session exit; existing time_only family uses absolute durations only. Math caveat: novel_dim `session=US` reads as 'while in US session'; if intended as 'before US open' the predicate is logically inverted (Devil flagged this) — synthesizer should clarify direction or accept the literal reading 'force-close at US-session END'.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: ~33% of trades open during US session; all of them get a session-end forced exit ≈ 6000 × 0.33 = 2000
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Compatible with all entries; effectively a calendar-time stop. Note: literal-dim semantics is 'in-session', not 'pre-session'; synthesizer should pick one and document.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Hybrid composition: time-baseline + premium_extreme failsafe. None of the 5 base kernels combine time-stop with a state-flag rip-cord — structurally novel.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: Across all trades (6000), ~13% see premium hit the opposite extreme within their time window ≈ 800
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Generic time-based hybrid; works on any entry channel/side.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Composition of breakeven kernel + funding_pct_top_decile rip-cord. Existing breakeven kernel only ladders on price; funding-decile transition as second-stage exit is novel.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: Conditional on price reaching TP/2 (~40% of trades = 2400) × ~13% see funding decile flip during remaining trade life ≈ 300
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Note: funding_pct_top_decile is a state flag (≥p90); kernel must interpret a transition OUT as the trigger — confirm during paper-shadow.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Inverse of X079 (exit_on_volume_spike) — exits on volume collapse rather than spike. Distinct trigger semantics.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: All trades 6000 × ~25% see post-entry volume drop into bottom quartile ≈ 1500
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Risk on Reserved6 entries: post-event volume collapse is structural; needs ≥90s post-entry trigger window to avoid clipping genuine moves.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Negative-side oi_change_pct entries are rare in the existing 257; combined with high-decile volume + high-liquidity gate yields a structurally new mean-reversion setup with naturally symmetric TP/SL profile.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: OI_Price events 1400 × ~10% (oi_change_pct ≤ -15 is rare on this OI-surge channel) × ~10% (volume top decile) × ~50% (high liquidity) ≈ 7
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Mean-reversion thesis pairs with multi_tp or fixed-symmetric (TP≈SL); avoid trail/runner kernels which assume continuation.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Two-leg negative-dislocation entry; current pricechange/long set hunts positive premium/funding extremes. Direction-distinct.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: pricechange 5000 × ~5% (premium_bps ≤ -20) × ~10% (basis ≤ -0.3 conditional on negative premium, partially co-incident) × ~50% (high liquidity) ≈ 12
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Structural-arb thesis: short-horizon mean-reversion fits multi_tp / fixed-tight-TP; trail kernels misaligned with thesis.

analyses.distinctness_notes: R6/long preview shows 50 archetypes collapsing to identical scores → existing R6 dims aren't differentiating triggers. Adding session+marketcap+hour dual-gate is the first R6 archetype with a real trigger-set narrowing.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: Reserved6 800 × ~33% (US session) × ~20% (large cap) × ~80% (hour 14-20 ⊂ US session) ≈ 42; conservatively 35
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: R6 short bias and US-large-cap focus → liquid execution; pair with fixed kernel TP≈3-5%, SL≈3% for symmetric profile.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Triple-orthogonal-confirmation entry: flow (taker_buy) × crowd (global_short_extreme) × cost (funding≤0). This specific 3-way combination absent from existing OI_Price/long set.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: OI_Price 1400 × ~25% (taker_buy ≥ 0.65) × ~10% (global_short_extreme p90) × ~80% (funding ≤ 0 conditional on crowded short) ≈ 28
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Squeeze thesis fits trail or multi_tp_with_runner — current results show trail family all-negative, so multi_tp may dominate. Note Devil's funding-vetoes-pumps concern: funding≤0 cuts most squeeze candidates.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Differs from E149 (pc_pump_aggressive_buy_immediate_long) by inverting side and adding smart-money-divergence (top_trader_position_ratio_dec). Smart-money-fade entry not present in existing fade set.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: pricechange 5000 × ~50% (event_type=pump) × ~25% (top_trader p25 dec) × ~20% (premium_bps ≥ 20 ≈ p80) ≈ 125
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Fade-pump thesis fits fixed-symmetric (TP≈SL ~3%) or multi_tp_with_be; avoid runner kernels — fade is short-horizon by design.

analyses.distinctness_notes: MATH DEFECT: novel_dim `weekday=Mon` is literal-Monday-only, contradicting stated 'skip weekend' intent (which would need Mon-Fri inclusion). As written, filter passes ~14% of trades; as intended, ~71%. Synthesizer must pick: either rewrite dim to multiple `weekday=...` rows or treat literal as 'Mon-only filter' and rename. Distinct EITHER way.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: If literal weekday=Mon: 6000 × ~14% = 850 trades pass. If intended Mon-Fri: 6000 × ~71% = 4260.
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Calendar gate; agnostic to exit family. Recommend the synthesizer fix the dim/name pairing before commit.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Liquidity-bucket-tied sizing rule; risk family of 41 doesn't currently have a slippage-realism dampener keyed to low-bucket bookings. Distinct.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: All trades 6000 × ~30% in low-liquidity bucket ≈ 1800 (each gets 50% size)
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Risk-side rule, exit-agnostic; will reduce left-tail magnitude under new mean/Kelly metrics — directly addresses R2 E126 paper bleed lesson.

analyses.distinctness_notes: AND-composite over funding_abs_high + premium_extreme; existing filter set tends to use single flags, so the conjunction is structurally distinct.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: Trades that match both flags: ~25% (funding_abs_high p75) × ~10% (premium_extreme p90) × correlation factor ~1.3 = ~3% of 6000 ≈ 200 SKIPPED
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Pre-trade veto; works with any exit family. Tight sample is fine since this is a SKIP filter.

analyses.distinctness_notes: PARTIAL-DEGEN: The cross-channel conflict semantics described in notes (opposite-side OI+pc within 90s) are NOT in supported fields — there is no temporal/cross-channel predicate. As parsed, archetype reduces to a `session=US` filter. Distinct ONLY if the loop has a separate cross_channel kernel that consumes the type metadata; otherwise this is a duplicate of any session=US filter.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: If parsed as session=US filter only: 6000 × 0.33 = 2000. If true CC-conflict logic exists: ~30 OI/pc opposite-side co-fires × 0.33 US ≈ 10
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Synthesizer note: confirm whether a CC-kernel reads the `type=cross_channel` metadata to apply the temporal conflict logic; if not, this archetype's behavior is the session filter alone.

analyses.distinctness_notes: PARTIAL-DEGEN: Same parser concern as cc_conflict — pc+R6 same-direction co-firing within 120s logic is unrepresented in novel_dim. As filter-parsed, reduces to session=US AND liquidity_bucket=high. Distinct only if a CC-kernel layer activates on type=cross_channel.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: If parsed as filter only: 6000 × 0.33 (US) × 0.5 (high liq) ≈ 1000. If true CC co-fire logic: ~20-40 pc+R6 co-fires × gates ≈ 5
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Same caveat as cc_conflict — synthesizer must verify CC-kernel exists; otherwise this is a vanilla session+liquidity filter.

analyses.distinctness_notes: First age-based filter; existing filters use marketcap/liquidity buckets only. Distinct.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: Most listed Binance pairs are >180d → ~75% of trades pass = 6000 × 0.75 = 4500
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Pre-trade veto on new listings; agnostic to exit family. Filters away unreliable kline regime — directly supports historical pattern validity.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Differs from X084 (exit_on_basis_invert) registered already by similar name? — verify slug uniqueness. SEMANTIC CAVEAT: novel_dim `basis<=0` is a state predicate, not a transition. If entry already had basis≤0, this exits at T0+ε for zero edge (Devil flagged). Need transition-since-entry semantics for the inversion thesis to hold.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: All trades 6000 × ~30% see basis cross zero during trade lifetime ≈ 1800 (much higher under literal-state reading)
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Carry-regime exit; pairs best with structural-arb entries (e.g. proposal #10). Under literal state-reading: mode of time-to-exit will be near-zero for entries already in basis≤0 — synthesizer should treat this as informative regardless and let the GPU loop reveal the distribution.
